title: saussurea dutaillyana var. macrocephala
intro: Saussurea dutaillyana var. macrocephala, also known as Himalayan Inula or Saussure's Inula, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of Saussurea dutaillyana var. macrocephala include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are often consumed as teas or used in combination with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
